HARTLEY H. BUSH
OF COUNSEL
April 11, 1990
Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel-
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
ATTN: 
John W. Wallace
Counsel- - Lega1 Division
Dear Ms. Donovan:
As City Attorney of the City of Riverbank, I am request-
ing formal- written advice pursuant to Government Code Section
8331-4(b). The question involves a proposed general plan
amendment, rezone application, 
prezone application, 
and
tentative subdivision map located on approximately B4 acres
in the City of Riverbank and property contiguous to the
City of Rj-verbank. The proposed subdivision, l-ocated within
the general B4-acre area, consists of 19 acres.
Councilman Carl- Lemmons holds shares in a California cor-
poration, Ambergreen, Inc., originally 
formed B to l-0 years
ago with nine shareholders. Over the years, various share-
holders have sol-d their interests to the remaining share-
holders and, in fact, the corporation has ceased to operate
as a corporation and the remainj-ng interested parties have
f il-ed income tax returns as partners.
In the year l-989
there were four 
"partners" 
and beginning with 
1990 there
are three, including Councilman Lemmons.
The Ambergreen corporation 
purchased a note 
secured by
a deed of 
trust 
on one-ha1f acre located within 
the 
84
acres in question. 
A residence was located on the property.
The owner of the property, 
Michael Riben, obtained a second
deed of trust 
from Martin Financial 
Company, which covered
the half-acre 
encumbered by Ambergreen and other 
property
contiguous to it 
owned by Riben. 
Mr. Riben defaul-ted on
payment of the second deed of trust 
and the horder of the
second deed of trust 
has been making the payments to the
t'partnerstt .


Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
April- 11, 1990
Page 2
The original 
note purchased by the Ambergreen group was
in the amount of $30,000. The house located on the property
burned down and sometime in 1987 the insurance proceeds
were paid to the Ambergreen group, which reduced the ]oan
to a point where the present balance is $7,094. 
Interest
on the note is at the rate of ten ( 108 ) percent per annum
and the monthly payments are in the amount of 
$272.05.
The hotder of the second deed of trust, 
Martin Financial,
has foreclosed on the property and has continued to make
the payments on the first 
to the Ambergreen group. 
There
is presently litigation 
between Martin Financial and Riben.
however, the Ambergreen group is not a party to that Iiti-
gation. 
Martin Financia.I is attempting to se11 the property
to the applicant for 
the general plan, rezone, prezone,
and tentative map, Liberty Devefopment Company.
Neither
Martin Financial nor Michael Riben appfied for any of the
entitlements requested from the Riverbank City 
CounciI.
Mr. Lemmons has earned interest 
income through Ambergreen
for the l-ast 12 months of less than 5200.
Question: 
whether Councilman Lemmons is 
precluded from
participating 
in the proposed general p.Ian amendment, r€-
zone appl-ication, prezone app.Iication, and tentative 
map.
because of 
his 
ownership interest 
in 
Ambergreen, Inc..
the record hofder of the note and deed of trust, 
with a
present outstanding ba.Iance of $7,094.
These matters are scheduled before the Riverbank Pl-anning
Commission on April 
L7 , l-990. and before the City Council
two weeks after that date.
I would appreciate hearing from you at your earliest 
con-
venience. Thank you for your cooperation.
Yours very truly,
City Attorney
City of Riverbank

5O9 WEST WEBER AVENUE
STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95202
MAILING ADDRESS:
PO, DRAWER AO
sTocKToN. CALIFORNIA 9seOr-997a
TELEPHONE {2091 94A-a200
May 23, 1990
Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 958L4
Attention: 
John W. Wallace
Counsel Legal Division
Dear Ms. Donovan:
This office represents Monschien Cabinets, an opponent
of a proposed residential development project in the City
of Riverbank. The City Council of the City of Riverbank
was scheduled to hear the applicant's request for a general
plan amendment and rezoning of the subject property on May
14, 1990. However, just prior to the hearingr w€ were
informed that a letter of inquiry had been dj-rected to your
office by City Attorney Thomas Hallinan regarding a
potential conflict of interest of one of the council
members. As Mr. Hallinanrs letter to your office correctly
indicates, councilmember CarI Lemmons is a part-owner in a
partnership which holds a note and deed of trust which is
secured by one of the parcels of property in question. 
We
have heard conflicting 
reports in recent days that
councilmember Lemrnons and/or the corporation has recently
divested itself 
of the note. 
However, during the time
period in which councilmember Lemmons' corporation did hold
the note, councilmember Lemmons voted against requiring an
environmental impact report in connection with the proposed
annexatj-on and general plan change on the subject
property.
We believe that both the previous action taken by
cotrncilmember Lemmons and any further involvement by
councilmember Lemmons in connection with this project would
violate the Fair Political Practices Act. First, the note
and deed of trust on the subject property was in fact a
source of income for councilmember Lemmons at the time he
voted to overrule the Pranning commission's recommendation
to require an environmental impact report on the project.
\
,- *_,
I


Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
ttay 23, l99O
Page -2-
Secondly, since the alleged sale of the subject note and
deed of trust is not yet complete, counciLmember Lemmons
has a direct financial j-nterest in a piece of property
which is being rezoned in violation of Government Code
Section 87103(c) and fPPC Guidelines Section L8702.L.
While we believe that councilmember Lemmons' previous
action on this property in connection rdith the
environmental impact report reqoirement negates the City
Council's action in connection with the EIR, it would serve
Iitt1e purpose for our office to bring any type of judicial
action to nullify that action. 
In fact, since no notice of
determination (CEQA Guidelines Section 15075) has been
filed in connection with the EIR requirement, we probably
corrld not bring an action until such notice has been
filed. 
we wou1d, however, encourage your office to
investigate this violation of the FPPC Guidelines. In
addition, $re would appreciate your prompt reply to the
request made by various parties with respect to future
actions which councilmember Lemmons may be required to take
in connection with this project. 
As you may be aware, the
city Codncil continued its hearing on this matter until
June 9, 1990. This office appeared at the May 14 hearing
in which motion to continire was granted. At that hearing
we contended that councilmember Le(unons could not vote on
the motion to continue (which he raised) since the Fair
PoIitical Practices Act bars the public official 
from
articipating in any way in a project in which he has a
@erest. 
rn your reply letter, we
would appreciate your comments on what we perceive to be an
additional violation of the Fair Political Practices Act.
In conclusion, we appreciate your office's work in
investiqating this matter. If vJe can be of any assistance
or if you reqoire further information, we would be more
than happy to assist you in whatever way possible. 
Thank
yoo for your time and your attention to this request.
Very tr rrly yours ,
4z-
THOMAS H. TERPSTRA
Attorney at Law
THT / ch
cc: 
Thomas N. Hall-inan
Frank Monschien
Elvan Overholtzer
